title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Detective Inspector Hakaste visits Mrs. Cording (also referred to as Mrs. Corton/Cottington) to investigate a murder at 19 Wilbraham Crescent. Mrs. Cording, who works for a blind woman named Miss Millechamp (also referred to as Miss Pemerton/Pemarhu/Pembroke), explains that she typically works there from 9:30 or 10:00 until noon. Hakaste informs her that a man was found stabbed to death in the living room of the residence.

The detective asks if anyone visited the house that morning. Mrs. Cording denies seeing anyone, specifically noting she would not admit a man claiming to be an insurance agent. Hakaste mentions a man in a dark suit who left a card under the name Mr. Corley. Mrs. Cording suggests the name sounds Indian, though Hercule Poirot clarifies it is not. The body was discovered by a stenographer who had arrived at the house by mistake.

Hakaste questions Mrs. Cording about the clocks in Miss Millechamp's sitting room. She identifies a grandfather clock and a cuckoo clock but denies the presence of a silver traveling clock, a gilded clock, a ceramic clock, or a leather clock labeled "Rosemary." Hakaste notes that four clocks at the scene are running an hour and a half fast. Mrs. Cording mentions that Miss Millechamp occasionally buys items like carpets and curtains at auction markets, though she does not usually buy small antiques.

Later, Dick Hakaste meets with Colin Lumb, an oceanographer. They discuss the upcoming trial of Rackin, who successfully smuggled important documents out of a secure location by having them photographed. Colin explains that the operation involved a flawless plan where a man used an identical coat to switch places with Rackin to avoid suspicion.
